zh.lifeSummary: 當Fulk de Corbet於1336年出生在英國史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特時，他的父親Robert Corbet32歲，母親Elizabeth Le Strange28歲。他於大約1360年與Elizabeth de Corbet在英國英格蘭史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1382年8月4日在他的家鄉享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

nameMeanings.name: Fulk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. The name was originally a short form of various compound names containing the word volk ‘people, tribe’ (compare modern English folk). It has gradually died out of general use, but is still used in certain families, in particular the Grevilles. Fulke Greville, 1st Baron Brooke, was a leading figure at the court of Elizabeth I. See also Greville .

variants.name: Fulke
meanings: Of Germanic origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. The name was originally a short form of various compound names containing the word volk ‘people, tribe’ (compare modern English folk). It has gradually died out of general use, but is still used in certain families, in particular the Grevilles. Fulke Greville, 1st Baron Brooke, was a leading figure at the court of Elizabeth I. See also Greville .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Fulk de Corbet was born in 1336, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, his father, Robert Corbet, was 32 and his mother, Elizabeth Le Strange, was 28. He married Elizabeth de Corbet about 1360, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 4 August 1382, in his hometown, at the age of 46.
